theme: Cutting parts the moment each order arrives
title: Commit-Cut: Streaming Panel Orders on a Kerf-Locked Sheet
innovation_hook: With irrevocable cuts, empty area is not fungible -- the policy must manage the shape grammar of remaining space and treat discards as options purchased against the known arrival mixture.
strategies: trivial: never discard, scan row-major from the origin for the first legal spot, no rotation, no lookahead

greedy (the trap): classic bottom-left-fill -- never discard, try both rotations, always take the lowest-y-then-lowest-x legal spot on the whole sheet; it fragments the sheet with kerf-locked slivers on cheap early orders and has no notion that a rare, value-dense order is coming

strong (the insight): classify orders by an adaptive value-density quantile computed from the instance itself, reserve a corridor sized to the kerf-inflated footprint of the value-dense class via a shelf-pack estimate, then replay the stream causally -- value-dense orders try the corridor first, everything else is confined to the general zone and DISCARDED rather than allowed to intrude on the corridor and kerf-lock away room the value tail will need

full local-search planner: refine the corridor-aware placement with post-hoc swap/relocate perturbations across multiple candidate quantile thresholds, keeping the best causal replay -- trades extra compute for robustness across interleaved, reversed, and held-out mixture instances
